name: Process documents with Google Drive and Airtable for your team

parameters.text: =🛑 Tonight's backup came out EMPTY — investigate immediately.
=✅ Process documents with Google Drive and Airtable for your team — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Weekly Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — weekly. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Export All Rows (Airtable)

### Export All Rows (Airtable)

Searches Airtable's 'Documents' table by formula (`={LastModified} > DATEADD(NOW(), -1, 'days')`) for records the pipeline hasn't handled yet.

Ports: ①→ Serialize to CSV · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Airtable PAT

### Serialize to CSV

Serializes items to a CSV binary file.

Next: Compress Archive

### Compress Archive

Zips the binary to cut storage/transfer size before upload.

Next: Upload Backup to Google Drive

### Upload Backup to Google Drive

Uploads the generated file to Google Drive as a timestamped archive copy.

Ports: ①→ Backup Size Sane? · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Upload Backup to Google Drive

parameters.content:
### Backup Size Sane?

Two-way gate on `={{ ($binary.data?.fileSize ?? 1) > 0 }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Save Execution Metadata · ②→ Alarm — Empty Backup

### Alarm — Empty Backup

Posts a message to Slack (`#docs`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Save Execution Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Align Field Names

### Align Field Names

Normalizes field names so every source feeds the same downstream schema.

Next: Sort Documents Newest First

### Sort Documents Newest First

Orders items (newest first) so limits and digests pick the most relevant records.

Next: Cap Batch Volume

### Cap Batch Volume

Caps the stream at 50 items to protect downstream quotas.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Airtable)

### Append Audit Row (Airtable)

Creates a new record in Airtable's 'Documents' table.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Slack · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Airtable PAT

### Send Summary to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#docs`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#docs`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

# Process documents with Google Drive and Airtable for your team

**Pattern:** File Processing · **Level:** Advanced · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Process documents with Google Drive and Airtable for your team. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual invoices is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Weekly Schedule → Export All Rows (Airtable) → Serialize to CSV → Compress Archive → Upload Backup to Google Drive → Backup Size Sane? → Save Execution Metadata → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to file processing without manual effort
- Anyone automating invoices in legal
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Responds in seconds
- Has retries and error handling built in
- Scales without extra headcount

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Airtable PAT; Google OAuth2; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

The empty-backup check exists because a backup that silently 'succeeds' with zero rows is worse than an obvious failure — it gives false confidence right up until the day you actually need to restore. Compression runs before upload, not after, cutting both transfer time and storage footprint in the same step.

## Practice exercises

You've got the basics covered — push further:

1. Point the export at an empty table on purpose and confirm the alarm fires.
2. Turn off compression and compare the resulting backup file size.
3. Add a retention step that deletes backups older than N days from the destination.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your invoices process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
